subject_commit_description: Merged Beneficial Genesis research base (main merge of PR #27)
receipt_self_binding_note: A committed receipt cannot contain the SHA of the commit that includes its own final state without becoming self-referential (the commit that writes this exact byte content necessarily postdates any SHA it could name for itself). This field set resolves that by splitting the work into two commits: repair_logic_commit is the commit hash containing the actual micro-repair code/doc changes (the validator, the governance rule rename, this receipt's non-binding fields), and receipt_binding_parent_commit is the parent commit that a small follow-up commit (binding only this receipt's exact hash reference) is built on top of -- which is the same commit in this two-commit pattern. The follow-up binding commit's own SHA is, by construction, never written into the receipt it produces.

repair_items_implemented.E-002_rebate_incidence: model/collusion.py: conditional (arrangement-exists) vs expected (access/enforcement/detection frictions) rebate incidence, side by side
repair_items_implemented.E-003_governance_conditionality: model/governance.py: 5 named rules (none/nontransferable_equal/nontransferable_proportional/token_weighted/cap_then_renormalize -- renamed from continuously_capped in the micro-repair below), opt-in per scenario via governance_rules, no default assumption
repair_items_implemented.E-004_mechanism_necessity_scope: MECHANISM_NECESSITY.md narrowed to charity+migration-receipt functions; added target-ledger-function matrix (SS3a) for unspecified functions
repair_items_implemented.E-005_implementation_and_metric_repairs: model/metrics.py: explicit share_of_pool vs share_of_issued, no mixed denominators; model/allocation.py: lottery draws without replacement; duplicate donor_id rejected fail-closed; assumption-asserting tests replaced
repair_items_implemented.E-006_language_boundary: removed 'token sale'/'investment contract' wording; replaced with 'fixed-pool floating implied allocation ratio' throughout
repair_items_implemented.E-007_complete_failure_condition_map: FAILURE_CONDITIONS.md maps all 7 issue #34 failure conditions (FC1-FC7, previously 6) each classified as mathematically_proven/conditional_simulation_result/structural_risk/empirical_unknown/policy_legal_question
repair_items_implemented.E-008_preserve_confirmed_findings: FAILURE_CONDITIONS.md 'Preserved findings' section retains all 6 REV-007 findings unchanged
repair_items_implemented.E-009_cross_model_and_repository_checks: CROSS_MODEL_COMPARISON.md added; all required commands run (see command_results)

micro_repair_items.1_shared_participant_validator: model/allocation.py: added validate_participants(), called directly by every public allocation function (exact_pro_rata, capped_pro_rata, concave_sqrt, concave_log, time_weighted, random_lottery_component); rejects duplicate/empty/non-string participant IDs, boolean/non-integer/negative weights, and invalid pool/cap/winner-count values; direct tests added in tests/test_allocation.py::TestSharedParticipantValidator (not relying only on scenario.py validation)
micro_repair_items.2_governance_rule_renamed_cap_then_renor: model/governance.py: continuously_capped renamed cap_then_renormalize; reports raw_proportional_weights, pre_normalization_clipped_weights, and final weights separately; tests/test_governance.py::TestCapThenRenormalize proves final normalized share can exceed the nominal clip value at 1, 2, and 3 holders; README.md, FAILURE_CONDITIONS.md, ALTERNATIVES_COMPARISON.md, MECHANISM_NECESSITY.md, CROSS_MODEL_COMPARISON.md, and the audit report updated to say only that this rule reduced concentration in the tested many-holder scenario (14_governance_cap_then_renormalize, renamed from 14_governance_continuously_capped), never a hard final per-holder cap
micro_repair_items.3_receipt_branch_tip_replaced: see repair_logic_commit, receipt_binding_parent_commit, receipt_self_binding_note fields above
